title: Waterfall-Framed Grand Console Table
summary: An 84"-wide waterfall-mitered console with a center divider splitting the shelf into two storage bays — the larger, divided sibling of this catalog's 52" waterfall console.
description:
This shares the same continuous-grain waterfall miter as this catalog's narrower 52" console — mitered 1x12 face frames wrapping top to bottom with no visible end grain — but at 84" wide, with a center divider that splits the lower shelf into two separate storage bays instead of one open span. That divider is what makes this the "grand" version: it's not just a longer cut list, it changes how the piece is used.

Build the mitered 1x12 top, sides, and bottom shelf first, all cut long point to long point at 45°, ends not parallel — this is the one step where the miters have to be genuinely tight, since a gap here shows on every visible face of the piece. The four inside-side/divider-side panels close in the two bays, backed by blocking that gives the side panels something solid to fasten into.

The mitered 1x3 face frame wraps the whole waterfall edge, front to back, with a separate 1x3 center divider trim finishing the vertical seam where the two bays meet. **Dry-fit every mitered piece before gluing** — on a piece this size, a miter that's off by even half a degree compounds visibly across an 84" run.

tools.note: For the 45° waterfall miters — a saw that cuts a clean, consistent bevel across wide 1x12 stock matters here.
For clamping the mitered corners while the glue sets.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° miter, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° miter, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Cut and dry-fit the waterfall miters
steps.body: Cut the top, sides, and bottom shelf with matching 45° bevels and dry-fit the whole waterfall assembly before any glue goes on — an 84" run shows even a small miter gap.

steps.title: Glue up the waterfall carcass
steps.body: Glue and clamp the mitered top, sides, and bottom shelf together, keeping the grain running continuously around the waterfall corners.

steps.title: Add the inside sides and divider
steps.body: Attach the side blocking, then the four inside-side and divider-side panels, closing in the two storage bays.

steps.title: Attach the mitered face frame
steps.body: Miter and attach the face frame around the full waterfall edge, then add the center divider trim over the vertical seam between the two bays.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: 84 inch waterfall framed grand console table with center divider
